graph_vector_fulltext.message:
I don’t have that information in the provided context. The context only says that there are “conditions under which an AI system is not to be considered to be high-risk” and that a provider who considers a system “not high-risk” should document that assessment before placing it on the market or putting it into service and provide it to national competent authorities upon request—but it does not include the actual conditions themselves.

If you share the relevant passage (likely the article/recital that lists those conditions, often referenced as the “conditions referred to above”), I can extract and state them precisely.
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_question: What are the conditions under which an AI system in a pre-defined high-risk area may nonetheless NOT be classified as high-risk?
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_answer:
I don’t have that information in the provided context. The context only says that there are “conditions under which an AI system is not to be considered to be high-risk” and that a provider who considers a system “not high-risk” should document that assessment before placing it on the market or putting it into service and provide it to national competent authorities upon request—but it does not include the actual conditions themselves.

If you share the relevant passage (likely the article/recital that lists those conditions, often referenced as the “conditions referred to above”), I can extract and state them precisely.

risk and has already been  placed on the market or put into service, in a way that the AI system becomes a high-risk AI system in accordance  with this Regulation. Those provisions should apply without prejudice to more specific provisions established in  certain Union harmonisation legislation based on the New Legislative Framework, together with which this  OJ L, 12.7.2024 EN ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj
----
 2016/679 or Article 3,  point (4) of Directive (EU) 2016/680 or Article 3, point (5) of Regulation (EU) 2018/1725. To ensure traceability  and transparency, a provider who considers that an AI system is not high-risk on the basis of the conditions referred  to above should draw up documentation of the assessment before that system is placed on the market or put into  service and should provide that documentation to national competent authorities upon request
----
 where necessary, the power to adopt acts in  accordance with Article 290 TFEU should be delegated to the Commission to amend the conditions under which an  AI system is not to be considered to be high-risk, the list of high-risk AI systems, the provisions regarding technical  documentation, the content of the EU declaration of conformity the provisions regarding the conformity assessment  procedures, the provisions establishing the high-risk AI systems to which the conformity assessment procedure  based on assessment of the quality management
----
 mandatory requirements for  high-risk AI systems laid down in this Regulation when adopting any relevant delegated or implementing acts on the  basis of those acts. (50) As regards AI systems that are safety components of products, or which are themselves products, falling within the  scope of certain Union harmonisation legislation listed in an annex to this Regulation, it is appropriate to classify  them as high-risk under this Regulation if the product concerned undergoes the conformity assessment procedure  with a third-party conformity
----

To ensure traceability and transparency, a provider who considers that an AI system is not high-risk on the basis of the conditions referred to above should draw up documentation of the assessment before that system is placed on the market or put into service and should provide that documentation to national competent authorities upon request

risk and has already been placed on the market or put into service, in a way that the AI system becomes a high-risk AI system in accordance with this Regulation. Those provisions should apply without prejudice to more specific provisions established in certain Union harmonisation legislation based on the New Legislative Framework, together with which this OJ L, 12.7.2024 EN ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj

where necessary, the power to adopt acts in accordance with Article 290 TFEU should be delegated to the Commission to amend the conditions under which an AI system is not to be considered to be high-risk, the list of high-risk AI systems, the provisions regarding technical documentation, the content of the EU declaration of conformity the provisions regarding the conformity assessment procedures, the provisions establishing the high-risk AI systems to which the conformity assessment procedure based on assessment of the quality management

mandatory requirements for high-risk AI systems laid down in this Regulation when adopting any relevant delegated or implementing acts on the basis of those acts. (50) As regards AI systems that are safety components of products, or which are themselves products, falling within the scope of certain Union harmonisation legislation listed in an annex to this Regulation, it is appropriate to classify them as high-risk under this Regulation if the product concerned undergoes the conformity assessment procedure with a third-party conformity
